name: Buffer
price_basis: Essentials at $5 per channel per month billed yearly, $60 per channel per year; basis is 4 connected channels at $20 per month, checked 2026-07
verdict_reason: KILL: $240 a year to queue posts. A scheduler is a cron job and a queue file on a machine you already own.
what_zo_builds: A posting queue you own: one plain text queue file per channel, a cron job that publishes at the times you pick, and a weekly report of what went out and what is coming. Editing the schedule is editing a file. Zo posts to X directly today; other networks run through their official APIs where they allow it, and the queue file stays the single source of truth either way. Buffer's own free tier already covers 3 channels, so the paid plan is buying you a meter.
dont_kill_if: You schedule for clients with approval workflows, or your business lives on Instagram and TikTok, where API posting is gated and an official partner tool earns its fee.
build_brief: Goal: replace my paid Buffer with a posting queue I own. Export my scheduled posts and connected channels from Buffer. Create one queue file per channel with post text, media paths, and send times. Set up a cron job that publishes each due post through the channel's API and logs the result. Add a weekly digest of published and upcoming posts. Run both systems in parallel for one week, compare what shipped, then cancel the plan. Done when a week of posts ships from my queue with zero misses and Buffer is canceled.
